rationale: The citing paper centers on intertemporal choice and temporal discounting in retirement-saving decisions, while the cited paper provides a direct mechanism-level account of discounting via subjective time perception and present bias. Given this strong construct overlap in the notes (discounting/intertemporal preferences), the citation could plausibly be a theory or mechanism source rather than a purely incidental mention. With only DOI-level match evidence and no in-text context, it is still safer to retain for full relation review than drop.
